TextENGNew:
Oda takes aim. His gun fires.
Rika is shot through the chest and dies in an instant.
"Waaaaaaaaah?! ...Rika-san?! Rika-san?!"
"Capture her. The surgery is urgent."
Mion snatches the blade from Rika's hand and braces herself.
"Don't shoot!"
"...Rika-san?! ...Rika-san?! ...Rika...san... ...Nngh...!"
"We need to do the surgery now! ...There's no time to waste!"
"...You killed...Rika-saaaaaan?!"
"...Calm down, Sonozaki-san! ...We haven't killed anyone! ...What you're seeing is a hallucination! ...You're in the terminal stage!"
"L-Lies...! ...You all...ki...killed her! ...I'll kill you! ...I'll kill you all!"
"Calm down, calm down! ...That's all a hallucination! ...Drop the knife!"
"...Shut up, shut up! ...I'll kill you, I'll kill you all! ...Raaaaaaaaaaaagh!"
Mion leaps at Oda and drives the knife into him. Oda crumples to the ground.
changeReason: Old translation added 'into his stomach' not present in Japanese; new translation more accurately says 'into him' matching the unspecified target in the original.

TextENGNew: "Why you—!"
"I'll kill you! I'll kill every one of you who killed Rika-san! ...Hoooooooooooooh!"
"Calm down! Calm down, I said! ...Sonozaki-kun! ...!"
Mion lunges at Nitta and stabs him.
changeReason: New translation specifies the target as 'Nitta' (matching the Japanese 新田) rather than the ambiguous 'him' in the original.

TextENGNew: ``You bastard!``
changeReason: Old uses 'bitch' (female-directed) for この野郎 which is a male-directed insult; changes the gendered tone of the exclamation.

TextENGNew: Okano grabs Mion from behind. In the struggle, Mion's knife catches him in the throat. Okano collapses.
changeReason: Old translation specified 'pierces his neck' and 'clutching his throat' which aren't in the Japanese (which just says the knife caught him); new translation is more faithful.

TextENGNew: Hotoda screams and tries to run. Mion catches up and drives the blade into her.
changeReason: Old translation specified 'into her back' not present in the Japanese (突きたてる = 'drives in'); new translation removes this addition.

TextENGNew: "Gaaaaaaaaah! ...Who killed Rika-san?! ...Damn it, damn it, damn it! ...I'll kill you! I'll kill you! I'll kill you!"
``...Sonozaki-san! ...Calm down! We didn't do anything! Honestly! ...You're...seeing things—you're on your own, and you're going berserk! ...Please calm down!``
changeReason: New translation adds 'on your own' reflecting ひとりで in the Japanese, clarifying Mion is lashing out alone in a hallucination, which the old translation omitted.

TextENGNew: "Shut up...! ...Give Rika-san back to me! ...Raaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaagh!"
She hurls herself at Hoshino and brings the knife down with all her strength.
changeReason: Old says 'stabs deep into her stomach' specifying both action and target; JPN 振り下ろす means 'swings down with force' — different attack action, and the old adds a specific body part not in the source.

TextENGNew: ...Bodies lie strewn across the stage. ...The downpour blends with the ragged sound of Mion's heaving breaths.
She comes back to herself and slowly draws near Rika.
"...Rika-san... ...Rika-san...! ...Please wake up...! ...It's time to go home..."
"Gyaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaah!"
The stage blazes red. Mion sobs and screams.
"...Poor child. ...It must have been so hard."
"Rika-san! ...Rika-san! ...It's all a liiiiiiiie!"
"...Hey, Mion-san. ...This time...I'll set you free."
"Let me see Rika-san! ...Please let me see her!"
``...You want to see her...? ...You want to see her, do you? ...You want to see her. ...Fufufufu! ...Very well. ...I'll let you see her.``
changeReason: Old uses 'them' (plural) where context indicates the person wants to see Rika specifically; new uses 'her' (singular) which better fits the narrative context, though JPN is ambiguous on number.

TextENGNew: "...Kondou-san. Kakinuma-san. ...Mori-san. Katou-san. ...And Arakawa-kun—they all saw her."
changeReason: Old translation implied Arakawa-kun met everyone; new translation reinterprets the sentence structure so all the named individuals 'saw her,' better matching Oyashiro-sama's listing of people who have died.

TextENGNew: "...Now you as well...shall see her, of course."
changeReason: Old translation used 'them' but new uses 'her,' consistent with the context of seeing the deceased Rika.

TextENGNew: "Let me see her! ...Let me see her! ...Rika-saaaaaaaaaaaan!"

A thunderclap booms.
The stage flashes bright, then goes dark.
Only the soft sound of rain persists.
A long silence.

The stage gradually brightens.
Only Mion and Rika remain.
Mion is sleeping with her head resting in Rika's lap.
"...Did you wake up...?"
"...The medicine still hasn't kicked in? ...You can sleep a little longer, you know."
"...Rika-san...? ...It really is you, isn't it...?"
"...Who else would I be...?"
"Rika-san... ...Rika-san! ...Rika-san!"
Mion wraps her arms around Rika.
Rika is caught off guard for a moment, then gently hugs her back.
"It really is you, right?! ...You are Rika-san, right?! ...Waaaaaaaaaaah!"
"...I'm here. ...I'm right here. ...Did you have a sad dream...?"
"...Nnh... ...Waaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaaah!"
Rika holds Mion with a tender, gentle expression.
"...Have you calmed down...?"
"Ha—hahaha... ...Yes. ...I'm sorry about that... ...Haha..."
"...Hey, Mion. ...I didn't want to wake you up, so... ...we let a bus go by without getting on."
"What?! ...Then when is the next bus coming?!"
"...You don't want to wait any longer, do you? ...Hey—why don't we just walk? ...If we take the shortcut, I don't think it'll take that long."
"...But we don't have an umbrella. ...Though the rain does look like it's let up a little..."
"...Don't you want to walk through the rain with me...?"
"Of course I do! ...For you, Rika-san, I'd walk through fire or flood! Let's go! I'll escort you wherever you please!"
"...Are you really sure...? ...Without an umbrella...we'll get completely soaked."
"Of course!"
"You'll get covered in mud."
"I'm ready for that!"
"...You might catch a cold."
"We're walking in the rain!"
The two of them stand up.
changeReason: Old translation said 'stand there for a moment' implying they were already standing; new correctly translates 立ち上がる as 'stand up' since they were sitting at the bus stop.

TextENGNew: "...I'll always stay by your side. ...So don't go anywhere without me, okay?"
"Ahhn! I'd never leave! I'd rather press right up against you!"
"...Yes. ...That's perfect. ...Let's go."
Rika takes Mion's hand and starts walking. Mion's heart flutters with nervous excitement.
changeReason: Old says 'holding Mion's shoulder' but JPN 魅音の手を取り means 'takes Mion's hand' — wrong body part.

TextENGNew: "...Wh...what's gotten into you, Rika-san? ...You seem different from usual somehow..."
"...Let's go. ...With the two of us together, we can go anywhere."
The stage gradually fades to black.
After a moment, the piercing beep of a radio call breaks the silence.
"This is vehicle three."
"...HQ to all units on standby. ...The target did not board the bus. Repeat—the target did not board the bus."
"Reconnaissance to HQ! ...The bus stop is completely empty. We've lost the target."
"...Block off all roads. ...Repeat—block off all roads. ...Don't let them reach the city! ...Capture them alive!"
